persons.arguments_against: The bio is flagged single-initial and the early staff-list rows print 'R. E. Turnbull', a middle initial the biography never gives — so the strongest case against is that the Governor was a different R. (E.) Turnbull among the 31 Turnbulls in the lists. This fails on every independent dimension: the office is Governor and Commander-in-Chief of North Borneo, a unique post the biography claims for exactly these years ('gov. and c.-in-c., N. Borneo, 1954-60'); the honours track in lockstep — the stint shows C.M.G. in 1954-55 and K.C.M.G. from 1956, matching the bio's 'C.M.G. (1946)' and 'K.C.M.G. (1956)' to the year; and from 1956 the list prints the full forename 'Sir Roland Turnbull'. A namesake hypothesis would require a second Sir Roland Turnbull, K.C.M.G. (1956), governing the same colony in the same years — not a live possibility. The 'E.' is most plausibly a middle initial omitted from the biography's heading (or OCR noise), not a different man.
persons.would_overturn: Evidence that the 1954-55 'R. E. Turnbull' was a different person from the 1956-57 'Sir Roland Turnbull' (e.g. a gubernatorial succession in North Borneo in 1955-56), or that the K.C.M.G. (1956) was conferred on a different Turnbull.

notes: Unique single-occupant post (Governor, North Borneo) with exact tenure match, honour-progression match (C.M.G. then K.C.M.G. in 1956, both attested in the bio with those years), and full forename printed from 1956. The mechanical position_sim FAIL (59 vs 60) is an abbreviation artifact: 'gov. and c.-in-c.' is verbatim 'Governor and Commander-in-Chief'.
